originalTitle: Europe's AI stocks shine through gloom of Iran war
summary: A strong rally in tech stocks has largely gone under the radar against a darkening backdrop for European equity markets as the energy shock triggered by the Iran war dampens ‌economic growth. The war has paused "Make Europe Great Again" trades, with the region's shares expected to underperform U.S. peers as ‌long as it persists. "The performance of our EU AI baskets since April is on par with the Nasdaq, just a touch behind Taiwan," said TS Lombard European and global macro director Davide Oneglia, referring to the U.S. index and Taiwan's bourse.
content:
LONDON, May 22 (Reuters) - A strong rally in tech stocks has largely gone under the radar against a darkening backdrop for European equity markets as the energy shock triggered by the Iran war dampens ‌economic growth.

The war has paused "Make Europe Great Again" trades, with the region's shares expected to underperform U.S. peers as ‌long as it persists. Data shows euro zone economic activity fell at its sharpest rate in more than two-and-a-half years in May.

Yet research from TS Lombard ​shows two baskets of AI-related shares account for more than two-thirds of the positive performance in European stocks over the past month and a half.

"The performance of our EU AI baskets since April is on par with the Nasdaq, just a touch behind Taiwan," said TS Lombard European and global macro director Davide Oneglia, referring to the U.S. index and Taiwan's bourse.

"Look through macro chaos and don't ‌ignore European AI winners," Oneglia added.

One TS Lombard ⁠AI basket, made up of firms in the semiconductor supply chain such as ASML, Infineon and STMicroelectronics, has rallied by roughly 20% since the start of April.

The other, up around 22%, is made ⁠up of companies in the AI infrastructure buildout such as data centres and includes Schneider Electric and Italy's Prysmian.

Gains in European tech shares are dwarfed by a 55% rise in the South Korean index over the same period, LSEG data shows. The Nasdaq 100 is up by ​roughly ​21% and Taiwanese stocks have rallied by around 28%, the TS Lombard ​report shows.

A renewed focus on AI, ‌highlighted by strong U.S. tech earnings since early April, as well as a push by Europe to support tech infrastructure suggest the European tech share rally could have further to run.

Seema Shah, chief global strategist at Principal Asset Management, which manages around $578 billion, noted Europe's renewed focus on innovation which has begun to show in the last two years in defence, energy security and AI infrastructure.

"You are seeing capital expenditure into those areas. We do think that those kinds of secular themes have remained pretty strong ‌and probably have been actually reinforced by the conflict," said Shah.

Even after recent ​gains, European tech shares have a cheaper valuation than U.S. competitors. The ​European tech stocks sub-index trades at almost 28 times ​expected earnings, compared with almost 35 times for the Nasdaq.

Oneglia said his analysis of European AI stocks ‌focused on the post-April period because that is when ​the AI theme resurfaced globally, with ​strong earnings and investors changing their minds on whether AI spending plans were excessive or not.

U.S. tech bellwether Nvidia on Wednesday released first-quarter revenue that beat Wall Street expectations.

While Europe's STOXX 600 index has fallen just over 2% since ​the Iran war began on February 28, ‌the region's tech shares have surged 10% and this week hit their highest level since 2000.

Tech makes up ​just 10% of the European benchmark, which is dominated by financials, industrials and healthcare.

(Reporting by Dhara Ranasinghe; additional ​reporting by Lucy Raitano; Editing by Alun John and Alexander Smith)
chineseTitle: 欧洲 AI 股在伊朗战争的阴霾中大放异彩
chineseSummary: 由于伊朗战争引发的能源冲击抑制了经济增长，欧洲股市黯淡的背景下，科技股的强劲上涨在很大程度上没有受到关注。战争已经暂停了“让欧洲再次伟大”的交易，只要战争持续下去，该地区的股票预计将跑输美国同行。 TS Lombard 欧洲和全球宏观总监 Davide Oneglia 表示：“自 4 月份以来，我们欧盟人工智能篮子的表现与纳斯达克指数相当，仅落后台湾指数一点点。”他指的是美国指数和台湾证券交易所。
chineseContent:
路透伦敦 5 月 22 日 - 在欧洲股市黯淡的背景下，由于伊朗战争引发的能源冲击抑制了经济增长，科技股的强劲上涨在很大程度上没有受到关注。

战争已经暂停了“让欧洲再次伟大”的交易，只要战争持续下去，该地区的股票预计将跑输美国同行。数据显示，5月份欧元区经济活动出现两年半多以来最大幅度下滑。

然而 TS Lombard 的研究显示，两篮子人工智能相关股票占过去一个半月欧洲股市积极表现的三分之二以上。

TS Lombard 欧洲和全球宏观总监 Davide Oneglia 表示：“自 4 月份以来，我们欧盟人工智能篮子的表现与纳斯达克指数相当，仅落后台湾指数一点点。”他指的是美国指数和台湾证券交易所。

“审视宏观混乱，不要忽视欧洲人工智能的赢家，”奥内利亚补充道。

由 ASML、英飞凌和意法半导体等半导体供应链公司组成的 TS Lombard ⁠AI 篮子自 4 月初以来已上涨约 20%。

另一个增长约 22%，由数据中心等人工智能基础设施建设公司组成，包括施耐德电气和意大利普睿司曼。

伦敦证券交易所数据显示，欧洲科技股的涨幅与同期韩国指数 55% 的涨幅相比相形见绌。 TS Lombard 报告显示，纳斯达克 100 指数上涨约 21%，台湾股市上涨约 28%。

自 4 月初以来，美国科技股的强劲盈利凸显了对人工智能的重新关注，以及欧洲支持科技基础设施的努力，表明欧洲科技股的涨势可能会进一步持续。

管理着约 5780 亿美元资产的信安资产管理公司 (Principal Asset Management) 首席全球策略师西玛·沙阿 (Seema Shah) 指出，欧洲重新关注创新，这一点在过去两年中开始在国防、能源安全和人工智能基础设施领域得到体现。

“你会看到这些领域的资本支出。我们确实认为这类世俗主题仍然相当强劲，而且可能实际上因冲突而得到加强，”沙阿说。

即使在最近上涨之后，欧洲科技股的估值也比美国竞争对手便宜。欧洲科技股分类指数的预期市盈率接近 28 倍，而纳斯达克指数的市盈率接近 35 倍。

奥内利亚表示，他对欧洲人工智能股票的分析主要集中在 4 月之后的时期，因为那时人工智能主题在全球范围内重新出现，盈利强劲，投资者对人工智能支出计划是否过度的看法发生了改变。

美国科技领头羊英伟达周三公布的第一季度营收超出了华尔街预期。

尽管自 2 月 28 日伊朗战争爆发以来，欧洲 STOXX 600 指数已下跌略高于 2%，但该地区的科技股却飙升了 10%，并于本周触及 2000 年以来的最高水平。

科技股仅占欧洲基准指数的 10%，欧洲基准指数以金融、工业和医疗保健为主。

（Dhara Ranasinghe 报道；Lucy Raitano 补充报道；Alun John 和 Alexander Smith 编辑）
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
